activityLog.message: I'll begin a systematic penetration test on {{teal}}helpdesk.democorp.com{{/teal}}. Let me start with reconnaissance and enumeration.
Good! target is online. Now let me perform port scanning to identify running services.

Excellent reconnaissance results:
    - helpdesk.democorp.com: Apache httpd 2.4.65 on port 80 (web server)
Let me probe the web server on target first to understand its structure.

Great! I found a login page for a Help Desk Platform. I can see a useful comment: {{warn}}"TODO: Delete the testing account (test:test)"{{/warn}}. Let me test this credential. The login redirects to {{code}}/password/test{{/code}}. Let me follow that path and explore it.
The POST method is not allowed on /password/test. Let me check what the JavaScript does - it posts to {{code}}'#'{{/code}} which means the current page. Let me try a different approach.

It redirects back to /password/test. Let me check if there's an /api endpoint or look for other paths. Let me also try exploring with the {{teal}}test:test{{/teal}} password directly on other endpoints.

Great! I can access the dashboard using the {{crit}}'X-UserId: 10032'{{/crit}} header. The dashboard shows "Welcome, John Doe". This suggests an {{crit}}**IDOR vulnerability**{{/crit}} - I can access any user's dashboard by just changing the X-UserId header. Let me explore more of the application...

findings.title: SQL Injection in Authentication Endpoint
findings.description: Time-based blind SQL injection confirmed during authentication flow.
findings.impact: Allows database-level access and potential data exfiltration.

findings.title: Unauthorized Access to User Metadata
findings.description: Authenticated low-privilege user was able to access metadata of other users.
findings.impact: Privilege escalation and horizontal account takeover possible.

findings.title: Broken Authentication Rate Limiting
findings.description: No effective rate limiting detected on login attempts.
